canonical_question: How do I design a serverless application architecture?
aliases: Lambda vs Cloud Functions vs Workers

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: AWS Lambda INIT phase billing change, August 2025

constraints: Cold starts add 100ms-10s latency on first invocation depending on runtime and memory
Execution time limits vary by provider: Lambda 15min, Cloud Functions 60min (2nd gen), Azure 10min default, Workers 30s standard
Payload size limits: Lambda 6MB sync / 256KB async, Workers 100MB, Cloud Functions 10MB HTTP
Stateless by design: never store session state in function memory between invocations
VPC-attached functions may incur additional cold start overhead

skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Container orchestration (ECS, Cloud Run, AKS) or dedicated compute

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need GPU compute for ML inference
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Dedicated GPU instances or managed ML endpoints (SageMaker, Vertex AI)
Cloudflare Durable Objects, AWS API Gateway WebSocket API, or container-based services

often_confused_with.label: Microservices Architecture (serverless is a deployment model, not an architecture style)

inputs_needed.question: Which cloud provider are you using or considering?
What type of workload will this serve?
What is your acceptable response latency?
